steps.title: Combine the coefficients
steps.content:
Factor out the shared expression $x+2$:
$$
\frac{1}{6}(x+2)-\frac{1}{5}(x+2)=\bigl(\frac{1}{6}-\frac{1}{5}\bigr)(x+2)
$$
Since
$$
\frac{1}{6}-\frac{1}{5}=-\frac{1}{30}
$$
the equation becomes
$$
-\frac{1}{30}(x+2)=-5
$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content: Multiply both sides by $-30$: $$ x+2=150 $$ $$ x=148 $$ The answer is C.
